With just over a month remaining in the 2025/26 Australian racing season, several jockey premierships remain undecided. While some leaders have established commanding advantages, others are locked in enthralling contests that promise to go right down to the wire.

Strong demand from a deep domestic and international buying bench fuelled an encouraging edition of New Zealand Bloodstock’s National Weanling Sale, with turnover, average and clearance rate all posting significant gains at Karaka on Thursday.

Trilogy Racing and Blue Gum Farm announced Libertad and Coleman would join the roster for 2026 alongside current stallions Flying Artie and Oxley Road. Libertad will stand his first season at $9900 including GST, while Coleman debuts at $7700.

Home Affairs will collect this season’s First Season Sire Championship thanks to G1 Golden Slipper winner Guest House among others. Can we use a stallion’s race record to predict who will be in contention for next season’s Second Season Sire Championship?

A powerful debut from Omolong set the tone for a productive midweek meeting at Warwick Farm, with the 2-year-old Extreme Choice colt announcing himself in style for Chris Waller, while Pin Up Sheila maintained her unbeaten record and Zoufame produced a dominant return to the races to pick up his maiden in style.

The countdown to the 2026 New Zealand Bloodstock National Weanling Sale is underway, with a compact yet quality catalogue of 114 youngsters (after withdrawals) selling this Thursday 25 June at Karaka.

Two foals from the first crop of Cambridge Stud's dual Group 1-winning shuttler Chaldean will be offered at Thursday's New Zealand Bloodstock National Weanling Sale, and their reception has the Cambridge team enthused for what the future holds for the stallion.

A far-reaching plan released to arrest New Zealand racing's decline was released on Wednesday, and it comes from seven of its most powerful breeders, investors and operators. From a pooled property vehicle to tax reform and a single governing body, the report asks whether those who own racing's assets are ready to put them to work for its future.
